conjecture_title: Conjecture on tightness of the upper bound for all r ≥ 3
paper_title: The upper logarithmic density of monochromatic subset sums
summary: Conlon, Fox, and Pham prove in Theorem 1 that $c_r \leq \left(1-\frac{1}{2b_0}\right)\left(1+\frac{1}{2^r b_0 - r}\right)$ for all $r \geq 2$ and establish tightness for $r=2$ (giving $c_2=(2+\sqrt{3})/4$). They conjecture the same formula gives the exact value of $c_r$ for all $r \geq 3$, but acknowledge their methods are insufficient. No follow-up paper resolving or substantially advancing this conjecture for $r \geq 3$ was found in an exhaustive web search covering arXiv through May 2026.
search_queries: monochromatic subset sums upper logarithmic density Conlon Fox Pham 2105.15195
monochromatic subset sums logarithmic density c_r tight coloring follow-up 2023 2024 2025
arxiv "monochromatic subset sums" r-coloring logarithmic density c_r exact value 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up found. The conjecture is open for all r >= 3. The paper was published in Mathematika (2022). Huy Tuan Pham's publication page and Semantic Scholar citation search returned no citing papers that address the r >= 3 case. The companion paper arXiv:2104.14766 ('Subset sums, completeness and colorings') develops the general techniques but does not resolve the tightness conjecture for r >= 3 either.
